right and moved 100 m. How many meters away he is now from his base station?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q6 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A person starts typing the numbers from 1 to 1999. He press the keys total ‘n’ number of times.
The value of n is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The number of times he pressed the keys for one digit number = 9
The number of times he pressed the keys for two digit numbers = 90 × 2 = 180
The numbers of times he pressed the keys for three digit numbers = 900 × 3 = 2700
The number of times he pressed the keys for the numbers 1000 to 1999 is 1000 × 4 = 4000
Thus, total number of times the keys are pressed = 6889

Q7 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Choose the most appropriate word from the options given below to complete the following sentence.
Based on his recent poor decisions, it was obvious that Seth lacked even a _____ of good sense.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Modicum is a small or token amount. It effectively conveys that Seth lacked even a small amount of good sense.

Q8 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Below is a sentence of which some parts have been jumbled up. Rearrange these parts which are labelled P, Q, R and S to produce the correct sentence. Choose the proper sequence. It would
P : that they were quite in the dark
Q : about the policy of management
R : appear from their statement
S : in dealing with the strike
The proper sequence should be

Correct answer: A
Solution

(b)
“It would appear ...” can be the only coherent beginning of the sentence. Thus the first part
would be R. Since R says “their statement”, sentence P would follow it because it also says, “that they ...”
Thus, option (b) is the correct answer.

Q9 MSQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

If you are a brisk walker, there is no need to go to a gym. Your neighbourhood can become your gym. You don’t need fancy equipment to get a good exercise either. All you need is a welldesigned pair of shoes.
The paragraph best supports the statement that

Correct answer: D, C, B, A
Solution

(d)
By stating that brisk walking does not require commute to a gym, the author stresses the
convenience of this form of exercise. The paragraph also states that brisk walking may result in a good workout.
Option (c) is not appropriate. A gym membership can be a good investment for some.
Option (b) is not given in the passage.
Option (a) seems logical but the paragraph does not mention it.
Option (d) is the most appropriate option.

If system uses write-through policy, then n is number of times C1 and C2 copied into main memory. If system uses write-back policy, then m is number of times C1 and C2 copied in main memory. The value of n – m is ________.

NAT
Correct answer: 18
Solution

18

Not attempted

Half duplex transmission means

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
It is not a strict condition that only ack is transmitted on other side. If other system is having data
to transmit, it can add data to the ACK, so that bandwidth can be effectively utilized.

Q20 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following are undecidable?

Correct answer: D, A, B, C
Solution

(d)
(a) Membership problem of recursive language is decidable.
(b) This can be decided because of finite steps (75).
(c) This can be decided because of finite steps (115).
(d) Membership problem of TM cannot be decidable. A string may half or not on TM. And halting
problem is undecidable.

Q24 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following SMTP commands is used to initiate the transfer of the email content?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
SMTP commands:
• MAIL FROM: Begins a new mail transaction and identifies the sender.
• RCPT TO: Identifies the recipient of the message.
• DATA Indicates that the email content will now be sent.
• SEND A rarely used command to send directly to a terminal-based user.

Q26 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Suppose a database has following relation instances:

 
Where underlined attributes are primary key.
Consider the following SQL query:
SELECT coach, Count (Sid)
FROM student S, Game G
WHERE S.Gid = G.Gid
GROUP BY coach;
The number of tuples return by above SQL query is ________.
NAT
Correct answer: 3
Solution

3
Query finds the coaches which associated with atleast one student and number of students associated with these coaches.
Only coach ‘PS’ is not associated with any student and total coach.
So, 4 – 1 = 3

Not attempted

Which of the following correctly describe the below ER diagram?

 
 
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
E1 to E2 is one-to-many and E2 with total participation. Total participation says every entity in E2
participate and one-to-many says atmost one. So exactly one association.

Q41 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following are correct?

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
• LALR(1) may have few conflicts, as compare to SLR(1) parser. That is why it is more powerful than SLR(1).
• LALR(1) depends on core of item (left part of item), not on lookaheads. So, same number of states as LR(1).
• On construction of LALR(1), an error entry in LR(1) can replaced only by reduction, not shift.
So, option (b), (d) are correct.

Q63 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following code segment of a program in C:

 
Which of the following is incorrect for above code?
Correct answer: D, C, A, B
Solution

(d)
(c) p + 1 = A + 1, which points to address of 2nd array of 2-dimensional array A.
(a) *(*p + 1) = 1 = *(*A + 1) = A[0][1] = 2
(b) *(**q + 1) = *(**&A + 1) = *(*A + 1) = A[0][1] = 2
